description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring a tan-colored upper and lower wing structure supported by white struts and bracing wires. The fuselage is streamlined and painted tan, with a black engine cowling at the front and a two-blade propeller. The wings display black cross insignias, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The tail section is rounded with a vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both matching the tan color scheme. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot.
description: A tan, vintage biplane with dual wings, black cross insignias, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing. It features a black nose, exposed fixed landing gear, and a streamlined tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, black engine cowling, two-blade propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled top-left view highlighting the black cross insignias on the wings and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cross insignias, and the streamlined fuselage and tail.
description_view_3: Angled top-right view showing the tail structure, wing bracing, and the open cockpit position.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and bracing wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
